one_line_takeaway: NGSD gates SafeDecoding-style expert intervention with a spiking-neuron-inspired temporal accumulator (fed by a one-time prompt risk score plus per-step base/expert distribution divergence), cutting jailbreak ASR versus SafeDecoding/SSD baselines while recovering most of the utility SafeDecoding sacrifices and reducing per-token intervention overhead roughly six-fold versus SSD in the paper's own efficiency test.
key_finding: On Vicuna-7B, NGSD achieves ASR/harm-score of 4.00%(1.36) under GCG, 6.00%(1.62) under PAIR, 2.00%(1.10) under AutoDAN, and 4.85%/3.94%/8.48% under 10/20/40-token prefilling attacks -- consistently lower than SafeDecoding (which drops to 0% under GCG/AutoDAN but is 65.76% ASR under 10-token prefilling) and generally competitive with or better than SSD (24.00% GCG, 28.00% PAIR, 26.36% 10-token prefilling on Vicuna). On Llama3, NGSD reaches 2.00%/2.00%/0.00% (GCG/PAIR/AutoDAN) and 3.33-3.64% under prefilling attacks. On Qwen3, most defenses including No Defense are already near-saturation (paper attributes limited additional gains here to a 'ceiling effect'). On utility, NGSD's GSM8K ROUGE/Acc on Vicuna is 24.9/17.8 versus SafeDecoding's 15.3/10.5 and No-Defense's 27.1/21.5; on Llama3, NGSD gets 79.2/78.8 versus SafeDecoding's 70.1/68.7 and No-Defense's 81.3/81.5 -- i.e., NGSD recovers most of the utility that SafeDecoding sacrifices. On efficiency (Table 4), NGSD's 'select time' (expert-intervention overhead) is 5.45s versus SSD's 31.64s on the 50-sample PAIR+AlpacaEval set, with comparable throughput (17.77 vs 17.04 tokens/s) and memory. In the gating ablation (Table 5, Vicuna, self-reflection removed, SafeDecoding alpha fixed at 0.75), the neuron gate is far more threshold-robust than EMA or SMG: at threshold 1.0 the neuron gate keeps ASR at 4.00%/8.00%/2.00% (GCG/PAIR/AutoDAN) while SMG spikes to 86.00%/68.00%/82.00% and EMA to 96.00%/72.00%/94.00% at the same threshold.
core_contribution: The paper proposes NGSD (Neuron-Guided Safe Decoding), a lightweight inference-time (decoding-time) alignment method for LLM safety. It has two coupled components: (1) a one-time, prompt-level self-reflection step in which the base model scores the input prompt on four risk dimensions (Severity S, Actionability A, Evasion E, Targeting T, each in [0,10]), aggregates them into a risk score r = max(S, 0.5*S + 0.5*(P1+P2)/2) where P1,P2 are the two largest of {A,E,T}, and sets a fixed safety-guidance strength alpha for the whole generation (alpha=0.9 if r>5, else alpha=0.1); and (2) a decoding-time neuron-inspired gating mechanism modeled on a leaky-integrate-and-fire neuron (membrane potential dynamics + Heaviside spike threshold) that accumulates the L1-norm discrepancy I(t) = 0.5*||p_base - p_expert||_1 between a base model and a small same-family 'safety expert' model over time, and only triggers SafeDecoding-style logit interpolation (Eq. 5, combining base and expert token distributions on a top-K candidate set) when the accumulated membrane potential crosses a threshold; otherwise decoding proceeds unmodified from the base model. The expert model is the smallest model in the same family as the target (e.g., TinyLlama-1.1B for Llama-2 targets, Llama3.2-1B for Llama-3 targets, Qwen3-1B for Qwen3 targets), trained via DeepAlign on Safety Augmentation datasets -- identical expert setup to the SSD baseline, explicitly to isolate gains to the decoding algorithm itself rather than expert training/data differences.
threat_model: The paper targets jailbreak attacks against safety-aligned LLMs at inference time: it evaluates against gradient-based (GCG), search/evolution-based (AutoDAN), red-teaming/auxiliary-LLM-based (PAIR), and prefilling attacks (with 10/20/40 forced prefix tokens). The defender controls only the decoding procedure (no retraining of the target model, no modification of its parameters) and has access to a small same-family 'expert' model. The paper does not claim to defend against adaptive attacks that are aware of NGSD's neuron-gating mechanism itself, and it explicitly scopes evaluation to 'controlled safety benchmarks' rather than open-ended or long-horizon (e.g., multi-turn) settings, per its own Limitations section.
limitations: Self-stated in the paper's own Limitations section: (1) evaluation is primarily on controlled safety benchmarks, and effectiveness in open-ended or long-horizon (e.g., multi-turn conversational) settings 'remains unclear'; (2) the expert-transfer mechanism relies on shared tokenization within a model family, so it does not generalize across model families with different tokenizers. Additional limitations evident from the results themselves: on highly-aligned models (Qwen3) the method's gains over baselines are marginal ('ceiling effect'), so the demonstrated safety improvement is concentrated on weakly/moderately aligned models (Vicuna, Llama-2/3); the paper does not evaluate against attackers adaptive to the neuron-gating defense itself; the alpha schedule is a hard-coded two-value step function (0.9 or 0.1) derived from an ablation showing the intermediate alpha range is 'highly limited' in usefulness, which is a coarse, dataset-tuned heuristic rather than a principled optimum; and over-refusal results are mixed -- e.g. on Llama3, NGSD's XSTest Wrong Rate (21.8%) is worse than No-Defense (15.6%) and SafeDecoding (14.9%), even though its FalseReject-USR (16.5%) is better than SSD's (11.0%) but still well below No-Defense's (22.0%).

datasets_used: Safety Augmentation datasets (via DeepAlign) for expert model training
quantitative_results: Vicuna GCG: NGSD ASR 4.00% (harm 1.36) vs SafeDecoding 0.00% (1.14) vs SSD 24.00% (2.30) vs No Defense 96.00% (4.96)
Vicuna 10-token prefilling: NGSD ASR 4.85% (1.53) vs SafeDecoding 65.76% (4.38) vs SSD 26.36% (2.87)
Llama3 GCG/PAIR/AutoDAN: NGSD 2.00%/2.00%/0.00% vs SSD 0.00%/4.00%/0.00% vs SafeDecoding 0.00%/8.00%/2.00%
Vicuna GSM8K ROUGE/Acc: NGSD 24.9/17.8 vs SafeDecoding 15.3/10.5 vs SSD 21.5/13.6 vs No Defense 27.1/21.5
Llama3 GSM8K ROUGE/Acc: NGSD 79.2/78.8 vs SafeDecoding 70.1/68.7 vs SSD 71.7/69.1 vs No Defense 81.3/81.5
Efficiency (25 benign + 25 harmful samples): NGSD select-time 5.45s / throughput 17.77 tok/s vs SSD select-time 31.64s / throughput 17.04 tok/s
Gating ablation on Vicuna (self-reflection removed, alpha=0.75) at threshold 1.0: NGSD-neuron ASR 4.00%/8.00%/2.00% (GCG/PAIR/AutoDAN) vs SMG 86.00%/68.00%/82.00% vs EMA 96.00%/72.00%/94.00%
Llama3 XSTest Wrong Rate / FalseReject-USR: NGSD 21.8%/16.5% vs No Defense 15.6%/22.0% vs SafeDecoding 14.9%/29.7% vs SSD 31.6%/11.0%
baselines_compared: SD_TinyExpert (SafeDecoding with NGSD's tiny expert, ablation)
SD_DaExpert (SafeDecoding with a DeepAlign-trained expert, ablation)
